title: containers cannot be reached after systemd-252-55 upgrade on EL9
body:
### Issue Description

I have a couple of machines with EL 9.7 and podman.
While updating via dnf automatic, my containers became unreachable from outside the host.

I've tracked down the issue to systemd-252-55.el9_7.7 packages (see dnf.log)

Since then, when rebooting, containers are unreachable from outside the host until I launch `podman network reset --all`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Have a podman container with a bound tcp port accessed from outside
2. update to systemd-252-55



### Describe the results you received

I can curl into my containers locally, but not from the outside, regardless of firewalld status or rules.


### Describe the results you expected

I should be able to curl into my container from another host in the same network.

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional information

This also happens on EL 9.6 as long as systemd-rpm-macros-252-55.el9_7.7.alma.1.noarch is installed (yes, el9_7.7 package gets installed by dnf automatic on EL 9.6).

PS: Sorry if that bug report should go into systemd instead of podman perhaps.

comments.body:
Note upstream only support the latest versions, and with older versions or distro specific issues like this it is best to file the bugs through the appropriate distro bug trackers. That said it sounds like this has anything to do with podman.

Now as for debugging there are like two likely issues, either the firewall rules got flushed (easy to check by listing the rules) or the ip_forwarding sysctl got reset. So most likely it is just a host configuration change in the systemd update, does it turn of ip_forwarding by any chance?
Note that starting with netavark 1.16 we should be creating config in /run/sysctl.d that write our configured values so that should taker account unless you have higher priory values set somewhere

Indeed I had `net.ipv4.ip_forward=0` configured in `/etc/sysctl.conf` on my systems (security hardened by default).

EL9.6 systems have `netavark-1.14.1-1.el9_6.x86_64` installed.
EL9.7 systems have `netavark-1.16.0-1.el9.x86_64` installed, and indeed expose `/run/sysctl.d`.

`net.ipv4.ip_forward=0` has been overriden until now, but systemd-252-55 must have changed the order / precedence.
Reading into [systemd docs](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/sysctl.d.html), I couldn't find any place where the value must have been overriden until now.

Both systems updated to EL9.7 and restarted, one will get the foward value overriden to 1, and the other doesn't.
Both sysctl dirs are identical, no idea why the sysctl order is different.
Anyway, this even happens in one of my collegues system with docker in EL9.6, so that's absolutely not a podman issue of course.

Thank you for your quick response.
